name: Macadamia Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. To toast macadamia nuts, spread them in a single layer on a shallow baking pan.
2. Toast in a 350°F oven for about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Set aside 2 tablespoons to sprinkle on top of the fudge.
3. Line a 10x6x2-inch baking dish with foil, extending it over the edges of the dish. Butter the foil; set aside.
4. Butter the sides of a heavy 3-quart saucepan.
5. In the saucepan, combine sugar, milk, and corn syrup. Cook over medium-high heat until boiling, stirring constantly to dissolve the sugar (about 8 minutes).
6. Avoid splashing the mixture on the sides of the pan. Attach a candy thermometer to the pan.
7. Cook over medium-low heat until the thermometer reads 236°F, or the soft-ball stage (25 to 35 minutes). Stir frequently. Remove from heat; add margarine and vanilla. Do not stir.
8. Allow the fudge to cool without stirring until it reaches lukewarm temperature (110°F).
9. Remove the thermometer.
10. Beat vigorously with a wooden spoon until the fudge begins to thicken. Add nuts.
11. Continue beating until the fudge becomes very thick and starts to lose its gloss (about 8 minutes). Quickly turn the mixture into the prepared dish. Sprinkle with reserved nuts.
12. Allow the fudge to cool completely, then lift it out of the dish with foil and cut into squares. Store tightly covered. Makes 60 pieces.
